title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' directed by Bradley Cooper stars Will Arnett as a stand-up comedian processing his feelings, streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' follows Tommy Shelby facing Nazi threats in 1940s Birmingham, streaming on Netflix.
'Jury Duty: Company Retreat' is a hidden camera comedy featuring Anthony Norman at a company retreat, streaming on Prime Video.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora is approaching eighth season as Red Sox manager with contract through 2027 earning over $7 million annually.
Cora has 610 wins; he needs 134 more to tie Terry Francona for second-most wins by a Red Sox manager.
Cora remains undecided about managing beyond 2027, prioritizing family considerations and team's current status over future plans.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line, featuring Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt, has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
Boston's second line has achieved a +19 goal differential, second only to Colorado's top line in the NHL this year.
Pavel Zacha has posted 51 points in 66 games, while Arvidsson and Mittelstadt are also having standout seasons for Boston.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, a 37-year-old from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH's pediatric cancer care.
Mahr was inspired in 2014 by young MGH patients and aims to raise funds for research and support families facing cancer.
Pediatric cancer research receives less funding than adult cancer, motivating Mahr's participation to bring hope to affected children and families.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The proposed Massachusetts income tax cut from 5% to 4% could save the median household $1,250 annually.
Tufts University analysis estimates the tax cut would reduce state revenue by approximately $5.1 billion per year.
Gov. Maura Healey opposes the measure, citing potential harm to state finances and economic competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church stands trial for allegedly poisoning Leroy Fowler with antifreeze over his affair in a love triangle case.
Prosecutors claim Church joked about poisoning Fowler and was the only one with access during the incident.
Defense argues Church filmed Fowler to show substance abuse effects and claims poisoned Powerade theory lacks evidence.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes legislation to lift Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban, allowing hunters to use crossbows.
The changes aim to manage deer overpopulation, aligning Massachusetts with other states' hunting laws, while opening 40,000 acres for bowhunting.
MassWildlife and Mass Audubon support the proposal, viewing it as a step towards effective wildlife and forest conservation.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a derringer pistol in a sealed case while magnet fishing in Bridgewater.
The YouTube video of the discovery by DeMontigny has gained more than 1,700 views since March 12.
DeMontigny's find quickly gained traction online, amassing about 1.2 million views on a subreddit.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old named Sergio Josue Castellanos is charged with murder after a stabbing incident in Chelsea.
Chelsea police responded to the stabbing report at 6:45 p.m., finding multiple victims near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue.
A 25-year-old victim from Chelsea died during surgery, while the investigation into the stabbing is ongoing.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two Boston police officers were injured during a struggle with a knife-wielding man at Forest Hills MBTA station.
The suspect attempted to stab bus tires and grabbed for an officer's weapon, leading to a gun being fired.
The upper busway at Forest Hills was temporarily closed, with normal service resuming around 1:30 p.m.
